title: Dryopteris kungiana
taxonomy: Dryopteris kungiana is classified in the kingdom Plantae, division Polypodiophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, and family Dryopteridaceae. It is a species of fern. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant. The family Dryopteridaceae is a group of ferns that share similar morphological features. The classification of Dryopteris kungiana is used in botany and taxonomy to identify and distinguish it from other plants.

intro: Dryopteris kungiana, also known as Kung's lady fern, is a species of fern native to China, characterized by its delicate fronds and unique growth habits. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to promote digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and boost the immune system due to its potential health benefits. Bioactive constituents of Dryopteris kungiana include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Dryopteris kungiana include teas, infusions, and dried extracts, often used to treat a range of health conditions and promote overall well-being.
